Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, indicating possible diabetic nephropathy or acute metabolic decompensation. Alkaline phosphatase is mildly elevated, and urine is abnormal in appearance and color. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal function, glycemic control, and possible underlying metabolic or hepatic disorders.
